title: Fiscal Year 2025 Veterans Affairs Major Medical Facility Authorization Act
short_title: Fiscal Year 2025 Veterans Affairs Major Medical Facility Authorization Act
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs.
plain_summary: This bill gives permission to the Department of Veterans Affairs to build and fix up medical facilities for veterans in two places during 2025. The two locations are West Los Angeles, California and Dallas, Texas. Congress is allowing them to spend up to one billion five hundred sixty seven million dollars total on these two big construction projects. The money will help create better medical care buildings for veterans who need health services.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Fiscal Year 2025 Veterans Affairs Major Medical Facility Authorization Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Authorization of Major Medical Facility Projects of Department of Veterans Affairs for Fiscal Year 2025
sections.text: The Secretary of Veterans Affairs is allowed to carry out the following major medical facility projects during fiscal year 2025 (October 1, 2024 to September 30, 2025). Each project has a location and maximum spending limit. First, in West Los Angeles, California, the Secretary can build a new critical care center and central utility plant, tear down old buildings, and renovate building 500. This project cannot cost more than one billion four hundred sixty million six hundred thousand dollars. Second, in Dallas, Texas, the Secretary can expand clinical space for mental health services, expand parking facilities, and buy land. This project cannot cost more than one hundred six million four hundred thousand dollars. Congress authorizes up to one billion five hundred sixty seven million dollars to be appropriated to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs for fiscal year 2025 or whenever funds are provided for the Construction Major Projects account to pay for these projects.
